evals.prompt: Apply tokens to the Stepper component in our Figma file WU01oSRfSHpOxUn3ThcvC5. The stepper component tokens are in tokens/component/stepper.json and the semantic colors are in tokens/semantic/colorLight.json. Create all the Figma variables needed and bind them to the component variants.
evals.expected_output: Should read stepper.json and colorLight.json, resolve the alias chain, compute any HSL modifiers via Python, create Figma variables with proper alias references, and describe the binding plan for each variant property (fill, text, icon, border, spacing).

assertions.name: reads_token_files
assertions.description: Reads stepper.json and colorLight.json to understand token structure

assertions.name: runs_python_resolver
assertions.description: Uses Python (resolve_tokens.py or inline colorsys) to compute HSL modifiers rather than trying to do it in Figma JS

assertions.name: uses_variable_alias
assertions.description: Creates alias references (VARIABLE_ALIAS) for pure reference tokens, not flat computed values

assertions.name: uses_figma_mcp
assertions.description: Uses mcp__plugin_figma_figma__use_figma for Figma operations, not a local plugin approach

assertions.name: correct_variable_naming
assertions.description: Uses / separator for Figma variable names (not . dot-notation)

assertions.name: sets_shared_plugin_data
assertions.description: Sets Token Studio sharedPluginData with dot-notation paths

evals.prompt: Create Figma variables for the Badge component tokens. The badge spacing tokens are in tokens/component/spacing/desktop.json under the badge key. The Figma file key is WU01oSRfSHpOxUn3ThcvC5. Just create the variables for now, don't bind them yet.
evals.expected_output: Should resolve badge spacing tokens (blockPadding, inlinePadding, gap, minHeight) and their dependencies (core dimensions), create variables in the right collections (Component for badge, Global for core), using VARIABLE_ALIAS for the alias chain.

assertions.name: identifies_badge_tokens
assertions.description: Correctly identifies badge.spacing.blockPadding, inlinePadding, gap, minHeight tokens

assertions.name: resolves_dependencies
assertions.description: Also creates the Global tier variables (core/dimension/*) that badge tokens reference

assertions.name: correct_collections
assertions.description: Puts badge tokens in Component collection and core dimensions in Global collection

assertions.name: respects_phase1_only
assertions.description: Only creates variables, does not attempt to bind them to component variants

evals.prompt: I need to bind the secondary variant hover state tokens to the Button component in Figma. File key WU01oSRfSHpOxUn3ThcvC5, component set node 29422:3597. Assume the variables already exist. Just do the bindings for Variant=Secondary, State=Hover across all icon positions.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the correct semantic tokens for secondary hover (color.interactive.secondary.fill.hover, text.hover, icon.hover, border.hover), find the right variant nodes, apply paint bindings with actual resolved hex colors (not black), and set sharedPluginData.

assertions.name: correct_token_paths
assertions.description: Uses color.interactive.secondary.fill.hover, text.hover, icon.hover, border.hover token paths

assertions.name: actual_hex_in_paint
assertions.description: Uses actual resolved hex colors in base paint, not {r:0,g:0,b:0} black

assertions.name: filters_variants_correctly
assertions.description: Only targets Variant=Secondary, State=Hover variants (including None/Left/Right icon positions, excluding isLoading=True)

assertions.name: handles_icon_drill
assertions.description: Drills into INSTANCE > VECTOR for icon binding, skipping Loading Spinner
